title: Post-incident trust rebuild memo
description: A data breach affected 2,000 patient records. Write the memo to customers, regulators, and the internal team that rebuilds trust.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at CareMesh, a Series C healthcare platform company (420 employees, $41.0M ARR). Your immediate assignment is post-incident trust rebuild memo. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Choose the smallest plan that can prove value without increasing operational risk.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 73101
New logo adds: 2576
Activation rate: 25%
30-day retention: 51%
NPS: 21 (n=44)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 25%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 9 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 8 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Mina Lopez): "Commercial momentum is fragile right now. Ambiguity in the plan will hurt conversion."
VP Engineering (Priya Brooks): "We can move quickly if every blocker has an owner and fallback path."
Head of Customer Success (Rohan Brooks): "High-noise accounts dominate feedback threads, but silent churn in mid-market is the bigger risk."
Commercial note: 2 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 26 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Atlas Commerce (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Mariner Foods (IT Manager): "Metric definitions changed between reports, which reduced confidence in decision meetings."
Keystone Legal (Director of Product): "We do not need every advanced control on day one, we need momentum in hour one."
Data gap: no structured interviews from churned accounts in the last 30 days.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 47
Median cycle time: 17 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 49%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 4
Release rollback events last quarter: 1
Average time to resolve escalations: 58 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 25% and retention is 51%, while NPS (21, n=44) and 26 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Mina Lopez around the 2 deal pipeline, technical caution from Priya Brooks on dependency risk (4 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Rohan Brooks due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 73101 active accounts, activation 25%, retention 51%, and on-time delivery 49%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
